시편 78:1.content: Hear my teaching, my people. Turn your ears to the words of my mouth.

시편 78:2.content: I will open my mouth in a parable. I will utter dark sayings of old,

시편 78:3.content: Which we have heard and known, and our fathers have told us.

시편 78:4.content: We will not hide them from their children, telling to the generation to come the praises of Yahweh, his strength, and his wondrous deeds that he has done.

시편 78:5.content: For he established a covenant in Jacob, and appointed a teaching in Israel, which he commanded our fathers, that they should make them known to their children;

시편 78:6.content: that the generation to come might know, even the children who should be born; who should arise and tell their children,

시편 78:7.content: that they might set their hope in God, and not forget God’s deeds, but keep his commandments,

시편 78:8.content: and might not be as their fathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation, a generation that didn’t make their hearts loyal, whose spirit was not steadfast with God.

시편 78:9.content: The children of Ephraim, being armed and carrying bows, turned back in the day of battle.

시편 78:10.content: They didn’t keep God’s covenant, and refused to walk in his law.

시편 78:11.content: They forgot his doings, his wondrous deeds that he had shown them.

시편 78:12.content: He did marvelous things in the sight of their fathers, in the land of Egypt, in the field of Zoan.

시편 78:13.content: He split the sea, and caused them to pass through. He made the waters stand as a heap.

시편 78:14.content: In the daytime he also led them with a cloud, and all night with a light of fire.

시편 78:15.content: He split rocks in the wilderness, and gave them drink abundantly as out of the depths.

시편 78:16.content: He brought streams also out of the rock, and caused waters to run down like rivers.

시편 78:17.content: Yet they still went on to sin against him, to rebel against the Most High in the desert.

시편 78:18.content: They tempted God in their heart by asking food according to their desire.

시편 78:19.content: Yes, they spoke against God. They said, “Can God prepare a table in the wilderness?

시편 78:20.content: Behold, he struck the rock, so that waters gushed out, and streams overflowed. Can he give bread also? Will he provide meat for his people?”

시편 78:21.content: Therefore Yahweh heard, and was angry. A fire was kindled against Jacob, anger also went up against Israel,

시편 78:22.content: because they didn’t believe in God, and didn’t trust in his salvation.

시편 78:23.content: Yet he commanded the skies above, and opened the doors of heaven.

시편 78:24.content: He rained down manna on them to eat, and gave them food from the sky.

시편 78:25.content: Man ate the bread of angels. He sent them food to the full.

시편 78:26.content: He caused the east wind to blow in the sky. By his power he guided the south wind.

시편 78:27.content: He also rained meat on them as the dust, winged birds as the sand of the seas.

시편 78:28.content: He let them fall in the middle of their camp, around their habitations.

시편 78:29.content: So they ate, and were well filled. He gave them their own desire.

시편 78:30.content: They didn’t turn from their cravings. Their food was yet in their mouths,

시편 78:31.content: when the anger of God went up against them, killed some of their fattest, and struck down the young men of Israel.

시편 78:32.content: For all this they still sinned, and didn’t believe in his wondrous works.

시편 78:33.content: Therefore he consumed their days in vanity, and their years in terror.

시편 78:34.content: When he killed them, then they inquired after him. They returned and sought God earnestly.

시편 78:35.content: They remembered that God was their rock, the Most High God, their redeemer.

시편 78:36.content: But they flattered him with their mouth, and lied to him with their tongue.

시편 78:37.content: For their heart was not right with him, neither were they faithful in his covenant.

시편 78:38.content: But he, being merciful, forgave iniquity, and didn’t destroy them. Yes, many times he turned his anger away, and didn’t stir up all his wrath.

시편 78:39.content: He remembered that they were but flesh, a wind that passes away, and doesn’t come again.

시편 78:40.content: How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness, and grieved him in the desert!

시편 78:41.content: They turned again and tempted God, and provoked the Holy One of Israel.

시편 78:42.content: They didn’t remember his hand, nor the day when he redeemed them from the adversary;

시편 78:43.content: how he set his signs in Egypt, his wonders in the field of Zoan,

시편 78:44.content: he turned their rivers into blood, and their streams, so that they could not drink.

시편 78:45.content: He sent among them swarms of flies, which devoured them; and frogs, which destroyed them.

시편 78:46.content: He gave also their increase to the caterpillar, and their labor to the locust.

시편 78:47.content: He destroyed their vines with hail, their sycamore fig trees with frost.

시편 78:48.content: He gave over their livestock also to the hail, and their flocks to hot thunderbolts.

시편 78:49.content: He threw on them the fierceness of his anger, wrath, indignation, and trouble, and a band of angels of evil.

시편 78:50.content: He made a path for his anger. He didn’t spare their soul from death, but gave their life over to the pestilence,

시편 78:51.content: and struck all the firstborn in Egypt, the chief of their strength in the tents of Ham.

시편 78:52.content: But he led out his own people like sheep, and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.

시편 78:53.content: He led them safely, so that they weren’t afraid, but the sea overwhelmed their enemies.

시편 78:54.content: He brought them to the border of his sanctuary, to this mountain, which his right hand had taken.

시편 78:55.content: He also drove out the nations before them, allotted them for an inheritance by line, and made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents.

시편 78:56.content: Yet they tempted and rebelled against the Most High God, and didn’t keep his testimonies,

시편 78:57.content: but turned back, and dealt treacherously like their fathers. They were twisted like a deceitful bow.

시편 78:58.content: For they provoked him to anger with their high places, and moved him to jealousy with their engraved images.

시편 78:59.content: When God heard this, he was angry, and greatly abhorred Israel,

시편 78:60.content: so that he abandoned the tent of Shiloh, the tent which he placed among men,

시편 78:61.content: and delivered his strength into captivity, his glory into the adversary’s hand.

시편 78:62.content: He also gave his people over to the sword, and was angry with his inheritance.

시편 78:63.content: Fire devoured their young men. Their virgins had no wedding song.

시편 78:64.content: Their priests fell by the sword, and their widows couldn’t weep.

시편 78:65.content: Then the Lord awakened as one out of sleep, like a mighty man who shouts by reason of wine.

시편 78:66.content: He struck his adversaries backward. He put them to a perpetual reproach.

시편 78:67.content: Moreover he rejected the tent of Joseph, and didn’t choose the tribe of Ephraim,

시편 78:68.content: But chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion which he loved.

시편 78:69.content: He built his sanctuary like the heights, like the earth which he has established forever.

시편 78:70.content: He also chose David his servant, and took him from the sheepfolds;

시편 78:71.content: from following the ewes that have their young, he brought him to be the shepherd of Jacob, his people, and Israel, his inheritance.

시편 78:72.content: So he was their shepherd according to the integrity of his heart, and guided them by the skillfulness of his hands.
